---
title: "Home"
---

<div class="hero-section">

<div class="hero-bg-overlay"></div>

<div class="hero-content">

<div class="hero-left">

![](images/photo_duke.jpg){.profile-image}

# Simeng Wu

### M.S. Student in Statistical Science, Duke University

<div class="hero-subtitle">
Statistical Modeling · Missing Data · Bayesian Methods · Applied Machine Learning
</div>

<div class="hero-description">
I am a Master's student in Statistical Science at Duke University. Before Duke, I studied Statistics, Economics, and Finance at University College London.

My interests span statistical modeling, missing data, Bayesian methods, machine learning, and data-driven decision making. I enjoy projects that combine rigorous statistical thinking with practical implementation.
</div>

[View Projects](#projects){.btn .btn-primary .hero-btn}

[Download CV](files/Simeng_CV2026.pdf){.btn .btn-outline-dark .hero-btn}

</div>

</div>

</div>

<div id="projects" class="home-projects-section">

<h1 class="home-projects-title">Projects</h1>

<div class="project-grid">

<div class="project-card">
<a href="projects.qmd#short-dated-options-volatility-forecasting-and-trading-strategy">
<img src="images/volatility.png" alt="Volatility forecasting">
<div class="project-card-overlay">Volatility Forecasting and Options Trading</div>
</a>
</div>

<div class="project-card">
<a href="projects.qmd#chess-anomaly-detection-with-metropolis-hastings-sampling">
<img src="images/chess.png" alt="Chess anomaly detection">
<div class="project-card-overlay">Chess Anomaly Detection</div>
</a>
</div>

<div class="project-card">
<a href="projects.qmd#web-scraped-recipe-dataset-construction-and-interactive-analysis">
<img src="images/recipe.png" alt="Recipe dataset">
<div class="project-card-overlay">Recipe Dataset and Shiny App</div>
</a>
</div>

<div class="project-card">
<a href="projects.qmd#insurance-subrogation-outcome-prediction">
<img src="images/insurance.png" alt="Insurance prediction">
<div class="project-card-overlay">Insurance Subrogation Prediction</div>
</a>
</div>

</div>

</div>

<div id="about" class="about-section">

<h1 class="about-title">About</h1>

<div class="about-container">

<div class="about-text">

My academic background combines statistics, economics, and finance, with a growing focus on statistical modeling for complex real-world data. Through coursework and independent projects, I have worked across time series modeling, Bayesian computation, machine learning, data engineering, and applied analytics.

I am particularly drawn to problems where statistical methodology and implementation need to work together. My recent projects include building volatility forecasting models for short-dated options trading, designing a Metropolis-Hastings framework for chess anomaly detection, developing an R Shiny application from web-scraped recipe data, and applying supervised learning methods to insurance subrogation prediction.

I enjoy turning open-ended questions into structured analytical workflows: defining the target, building reproducible pipelines, comparing models carefully, and communicating results through reports, visualizations, and interactive tools.

</div>

<div class="about-contact">

<div class="contact-item">
<span class="contact-icon">✉</span>
<a href="mailto:sw701@duke.edu">sw701@duke.edu</a>
</div>

<div class="contact-item">
<span class="contact-icon">GH</span>
<a href="https://github.com/SimengWu17">GitHub</a>
</div>

<div class="contact-item">
<span class="contact-icon">in</span>
<a href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/simeng-wu">LinkedIn</a>
</div>

<div class="contact-item">
<span class="contact-icon">CV</span>
<a href="files/Simeng_CV2026.pdf">Curriculum Vitae</a>
</div>

</div>

</div>

</div>

<div class="site-footer">

<div class="footer-left">
© 2026 Simeng Wu · M.S. Statistical Science, Duke University. Built with Quarto, HTML, and CSS.
</div>

<div class="footer-right">
Last updated May 2026
</div>

</div>
